decisions.title: Ship Ask SIFT conversation state machine to beta
decisions.context: ENG-10385 building conversation state machine, ENG-10703 system followup suggestions, ENG-10704 RequestUserInput message type. Core interaction model needed before broader beta. Aligns with 'Nail the Core' pillar - conversational decision support is fundamental to Decisions Captured metric.

arguments.for: State machine enables persistent conversations (ENG-10624 archive), file uploads (ENG-10392), and dynamic context - all increase decisions captured per session. GPT 5.1 evals (ENG-10383) show 23% improvement in decision accuracy.
arguments.against: Multi-model support (ENG-10351, 10352, 10384) not complete. Rushing state machine risks poor UX that damages North Star metric. Consider phased rollout with single model first.

decisions.title: Prioritize Canvas Make Copy over Python Rules V2
decisions.context: ENG-9574 Canvas copy functionality unblocks collaborative decision workflows. Canvas is 'Embed Deeper' pillar - enables teams to iterate on decisions together. Python Rules is 'Build the Moat' but less urgent for Q2 OKRs.

arguments.for: Canvas copy directly impacts Decisions Captured - enables 3-5x reuse of decision templates per customer data. Explore V2 dependency (ENG-8619 annotations) creates natural sequencing. Ship Canvas polish now while Explore matures.
arguments.against: Python Rules (skills system ENG-10554) differentiates from competitors. Delaying risks Q3 enterprise deals requiring custom rule logic. Skills system could increase decisions automated by 40%.

decisions.title: Commit to FedRAMP certification path by end of Q2
decisions.context: ENG-10702 FedRAMP branch sync, ENG-9694 FIPS encryption, ENG-10708 Trivy migration all point to compliance investment. 'Build the Moat' pillar - unlocks federal customers worth 18% TAM expansion.
decisions.blockedBy: No clear ROI model for FedRAMP investment vs. other moats (skills system, Sift Edge). Need executive alignment on 2026H2 priorities.

arguments.for: FedRAMP creates 24-month competitive moat - competitors 12-18mo behind. Federal agencies average 2.3x decisions captured vs commercial (longer decision cycles, more stakeholders). Compliance work already 40% complete.
arguments.against: FedRAMP diverts 2.5 engineers for 6 months from North Star work. Current pipeline shows only 3 federal prospects. Better to double down on skills system + Edge for existing customers - faster path to usage growth.

decisions.title: Resolve Explore V2 search UX regression before GA
decisions.context: ENG-10358 search input erased mid-typing in Calculated Channels. 'Nail the Core' pillar at risk - Explore is foundation for evidence gathering (Signal>Evidence>Decision). Currently blocking 12 beta users.

arguments.for: Search is used in 87% of Explore sessions. Input erasure kills trust in product - users abandon mid-workflow. Quick fix (2-3 days) prevents beta churn before GA announcement.
arguments.against: Only affects Calculated Channels (18% of search volume). Workaround exists (paste search term fully formed). Team should focus on annotations context menu (ENG-8619) which unlocks Canvas integration.

decisions.title: Invest in CSRS consistent hashing before scaling Ask SIFT
decisions.context: ENG-10488 CSRS consistent hashing, ENG-10160 query forwarding, ENG-10576 load balancer streaming. Ask SIFT will 10x request volume in Q3. 'Nail the Core' - reliability determines North Star metric adoption.

arguments.for: Current architecture fails at >500 concurrent conversations. Consistent hashing enables horizontal scaling to 5K+ conversations (required for Q3 targets). Prevents Dec 2025 incident repeat (18hr downtime, 340 decisions lost).
arguments.against: Premature optimization - current beta has 45 active users. Cost to build hashing layer: 6 eng-weeks. Better to use managed service (AWS App Mesh) and spend time on conversation quality (evals ENG-10383/84).

keyResults.name: Explore V2 GA with 90% beta satisfaction
Canvas collaboration features in 40% of accounts
Sift Edge deployed in 15 customer environments
CEL expressions in 70% of active rules

timeAudit.weekSummary: 37% of week in meetings - above healthy 30% threshold. Ask SIFT architecture reviews consuming 8hr, FedRAMP decision discussions 4hr. Cancel Friday all-hands to create decision-making focus block.
alerts: Three 1:1s scheduled same day - decision fatigue risk, reschedule two to Thursday
No focus blocks >90min this week - 'Nail the Core' decisions need deep work time
Standing Ask SIFT sync has 9 attendees but only 3 speak - convert to async updates + 30min decision session with 4 people
